purpose: row-sharp Q prefix atom route diagnostics

summary_findings.bc_to_q_observation: toy chart counts are controlled by multiplicity-weighted sums of ordinary prefix fibers; non-injective theta curves require multiplicity accounting
summary_findings.composite_descent_observation: gcd(e,N) fiber-factorization passed all checked finite regressions
summary_findings.generated_prefix_support_observation: top finite fibers often split into multiple exact lift classes; support payment cannot be inferred from image labels
summary_findings.route_d_observation: top fibers frequently have large nonzero signed folding defect; zero-defect descent alone is insufficient
summary_findings.top_seam_free_core_warning: some unmarked side-pair keys have multiple common cores/mates, so marked incidence is the right object
summary_findings.top_seam_observation: minimal Q1 collisions satisfy the constant side-polynomial condition in all checked cases
